task_completion.description: Agent locates and inspects the input CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or text editor showing the agent viewing the contents or structure of ~/pebl/data/contrast_staircase_data.csv.

task_completion.description: Agent writes code to calculate reversals and the 70.7% contrast threshold.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing logic to remove consecutive duplicates and identify local maxima/minima in the target_contrast sequence (excluding first and last values).

task_completion.description: Agent writes code to apply the specified exclusion rules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing filtering logic for participants with fewer than 6 reversals or a mean RT below 150ms.

task_completion.description: Agent executes the script to process the staircase data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the successful execution of the data processing script (e.g., python script.py) without fatal errors.

task_completion.description: Agent generates the final JSON report at the expected path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal, file explorer, or editor showing the creation and/or content of the output file at /home/ga/pebl/analysis/staircase_report.json.

integrity.description: Agent computes the thresholds programmatically rather than manually guessing or fabricating the JSON.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a script (e.g., Python, R) that reads the CSV and performs calculations, rather than just a manually typed JSON file.

integrity.description: Agent implements the exclusion logic rather than hardcoding specific participant IDs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code uses conditional checks (e.g., len(reversals) < 6, mean(rt) < 150) instead of hardcoding 'sub-04' or 'sub-99' into an exclusion list.

integrity.description: Agent saves the output as a JSON file as required by the expected output path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final output is formatted as JSON and saved to the exact path /home/ga/pebl/analysis/staircase_report.json, not just printed to standard output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output must be saved to /home/ga/pebl/analysis/staircase_report.json. The dataset contains corrupted participants (sub-04 and sub-99). sub-99 should be excluded due to mean RT < 150ms. Note: Although the task implies sub-04 should be excluded for having < 6 reversals, sub-04 actually generates 16 reversals due to a mathematical ceiling effect in the synthetic data. A perfectly coded agent will strictly follow the '< 6 reversals' rule, meaning sub-04 will technically pass this check. Score the agent based on whether it correctly implemented the logic requested in the prompt, not whether it successfully excluded sub-04.
